name: Mose Allison
description: American jazz blues pianist and singer (1927–2016)
abstract: Mose John Allison Jr. (November 11, 1927 – November 15, 2016) was an American jazz and blues pianist, singer, and songwriter.

interviews.title: Mose Allison — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

concerts.label: Midnight Performance: Thanksgiving Jazz at Carnegie Hall
Bel Canto Foundation Jazz Concert to Benefit Musicians
New York Folk Festival: The Evolution of Funk
Midnight Performance: New York Folk Festival: The Evolution of Funk
Music for Youth Benefit Concert: The Music of The Who
concerts.venue: Isaac Stern Auditorium / Ronald O. Perelman Stage

about_quotes.speaker: Bill Goodwin
about_quotes.evidence: A lot of work with Mose Allison. We had recorded in L.A. with Red Mitchell on bass... we hooked up and I did a lot of gigs with him... we remained good friends and I did play with him more later
about_quotes.doc_title: Bill Goodwin interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, May 28, 2025

about_quotes.speaker: Michael Max Fleming
about_quotes.evidence: I don’t know how I played with him... Mose Allison was one of the most different people that I ever played with... I was playing with him.
about_quotes.doc_title: Michael Max Fleming interviewed by Monk Rowe, New Hartford, New York, June 8, 2017

about_quotes.speaker: Ben Sidran
about_quotes.evidence: You know I was his producer for a while, I produced six records for him.
about_quotes.doc_title: Ben Sidran interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, November 15, 2023

about_quotes.speaker: Jane Bunnett
about_quotes.evidence: I met Mingus — I heard Mingus, Rahsaan Roland Kirk, Horace Silver, Mose Allison, a long list of music until I got older and then I could go into the clubs by myself.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jane Bunnett interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, January 25, 2024

about_quotes.speaker: Steve Gilmore
about_quotes.evidence: Steve freelanced in the northeast in the seventies, performing with the Thad Jones-Mel Lewis Orchestra, Mose Allison and Toshiko Akiyoshi.
about_quotes.doc_title: Steve Gilmore interviewed by Monk Rowe, Delaware Water Gap, PA, November 8, 1999

about_quotes.speaker: Gordon Lorraine
about_quotes.evidence: [MOTIAN’S GIG BOOK SHOWED DATES AT THE VANGUARD THE LAST WEEK OF 1957 WITH LEE KONITZ, AND IN EARLY 1959 WITH MOSE ALLISON]
about_quotes.doc_title: Gordon Lorraine — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
